page.content: The analysis presented in the following table offers a comprehensive overview of the relationship between various clinical outcomes and corresponding statistical measures. Specifically, the data highlights the significance of the metrics labeled as "CR" (complete response) and "p value," which are critical for evaluating treatment efficacy.

lines.text: The analysis presented in the following table offers a comprehensive overview of the relationship between various clinical
outcomes and corresponding statistical measures. Specifically, the data highlights the significance of the metrics labeled as
"CR" (complete response) and "p value," which are critical for evaluating treatment efficacy.

page.content:
The findings indicate a notable variation in the complete response rates across different study cohorts, as evidenced by the difference between the reported values of 106* and 93. Such discrepancies underscore the importance of understanding the underlying factors that contribute to treatment success, particularly in populations with distinct characteristics.

Furthermore, the "PR/SD/PD" (partial response/stable disease/progressive disease) category provides additional insight into patient outcomes, offering a broader context for interpreting the efficacy of the interventions assessed. The statistical significance of the results, as indicated by the p values, plays a vital role in determining the reliability of these findings. By examining these metrics holistically, researchers and clinicians can better assess the impact of treatment strategies and identify areas necessitating further investigation. The implications of these results extend beyond academic interest, influencing clinical decision-making and ultimately improving patient care.
